name: Blueberry-Couscous Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over and wash the blueberries, then set them on paper towels to dry.
2. Combine the apple juice, vanilla extract, and couscous in a large saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
3. Cook, stirring constantly until thickened and all the juice has been absorbed. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
4. Gently fold the blueberries into the hot mixture.
5. Rinse a 9x14-inch (inch) cake pan but do not dry it. Pour the mixture into the prepared pan.
6. Chill in the refrigerator until set, about 2 hours.
7. Cut into squares and enjoy without guilt.
Variations:
Use strawberries instead of blueberries, top with unsweetened fruit jam thinned with a little water, and arrange fruit slices on top for decoration.
